commit.message:
fix(ci): restore SECRET_KEY length and DEBUG flag for config validation

Settings.validate_secret_key rejects keys < 32 chars when DEBUG=false.
The previous commit shortened the key and removed DEBUG, causing the app
to crash on import before tests could run.

删除 ArticleComment 组件的引用和使用。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support coordinate-based bridge matching for validation data

Users no longer need matching structure_numbers in NBI. Three
identification modes per row:
1. structure_number → exact NBI match
2. lat/lon → spatial match to nearest NBI bridge (within 0.5km)
3. lat/lon + hwb_class → no NBI needed at all

Also adds --validation-template CLI for generating example CSV.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: sentinel None for config fields, gate validation, try/finally cleanup

- Use None sentinels for RoundConfig numeric fields so frontmatter-explicit
  values aren't silently overwritten by global config. Priority is now:
  per-round override > frontmatter > global config > default.
- Add _finalize_round_config to fill defaults and validate gate values,
  warning on typos like "hardd" instead of silently treating as "hard".
- Extract _execute_round from run_round with try/finally to guarantee
  _cleanup.current_round is cleared even on unexpected exceptions.
- Add truncation notice to static analysis output ("[... truncated]").
- Tighten CLAUDE.md test-command regex: remove overly broad "testing:" match.

commit.message:
README: Reorganize roadmap — defer Servo to Phase 20, prioritize VM and gaming

Tier 2 marked complete (Phases 15-16). Tier 3 reorganized as System
Completion: Phase 17 (Linux VM Bridge), Phase 18 (Gaming & Media),
Phase 19 (AI Marketplace), Phase 20 (Servo Investigation). Tier 4
renumbered as Phases 21-24 (Hardware, ARM/RISC-V, Enterprise, Kani).

Rationale: AI-Native Loom is core differentiator. Linux VM unlocks
developers. Gaming drives adoption. Servo deferred until core solid.

commit.message:
QA fixes: tab state persistence, plan gates, error states

- Tab state loss (main UX bug): replace single-render pages[page] with
  lazy-mount + display:none pattern. Pages stay mounted after first visit;
  switching tabs no longer unmounts them, so fetched data is preserved and
  there is no re-fetch flash when returning to a tab.

- CitationTrackerPage: restore real Pro plan gate
  (was hardcoded `isPro = true`, giving free users full Pro access)

- ContentDoctorPage: add 'agency' to isPaid check (was missing agency plan)

- TenantsPage: add 'starter' and 'agency' to planBadge color map
  (both fell through to badge-gray instead of correct colors)

- ScorePage: add retry card when score is null after silent API failure
- GeoPage: add retry card when geo is null after silent API failure
- Fix LS_URLS comment prices ($90/yr→$79/yr, $290/yr→$249/yr)

commit.message:
feat(habit): auto-detect repeated prompt patterns and propose skill generation

- UserPromptSubmit hook tracks prompts across sessions in ~/.claude/hooks/habit/patterns.json
- Jaccard similarity (threshold 0.25) with Korean tech term normalization
  (파이썬↔python, 파싱하는/읽는/불러오는 → 'read', etc.)
- Noise filters: skip prompts < 4 words, context-dependent phrases, skill-acceptance replies
- After 3 different sessions match a pattern: injects additionalContext so Claude
  proposes skill creation; user says 'ok' → Claude writes SKILL.md
- Caps pattern DB at 300 entries

fix: proxy Trestle media URLs, remove seed fallback passwords, drop unused xlsx - Create /api/media/proxy endpoint: server-side proxy for Trestle media URLs that require Bearer auth (browser tags can't send auth headers) - Rewrite all Trestle media URLs to /api/media/proxy?url= in public DTO, listing detail page, and CRM search response - Only allows proxying from known Trestle/Cotality domains (allowlist) - 24hr cache on proxied images (CDN + browser) - Remove hardcoded fallback passwords from prisma/seed.ts — now requires SEED_BROKER_PASSWORD and SEED_AGENT_PASSWORD env vars - Remove unused xlsx package (known prototype pollution + ReDoS vulns) - Reduces npm audit from 11 to 4 vulnerabilities (remaining need Next.js 16) Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add PD1/IL-15 fusion protein (SAR445877) intervention and hypothesis tracking

Model: PD1_IL15 intervention combines PD1 blockade with IL-15 effects
(CD8 proliferation via _attempt_division, halved CD8 death rate).
CD8 daughters inherit 50% activation, 30% exhaustion.

Sweep: Added PD1/IL-15 monotherapy and PD1/IL-15+CTLA4 combo arms.
1-seed test: PD1/IL-15 gives -13.8% tumor (vs -7.6% for PD1+CTLA4
combo), first intervention to expand CD8 pool (1290 vs 1084).
Triple combo (PD1/IL-15 + CTLA4-ADCC) gives -22.0%.

Notebook: Added hypothesis table (H1-H5) and experiment log.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redirect / to playground, add /vto route, default dark theme

- /vto serves the Virtual Try-On catalog page
- VTO nav link updated to /vto, hidden on mobile
- Dark theme is now the default for new users

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway): reject subscription credentials missing oauthData (#283)

Subscription credentials without oauthData would silently fall back to
reading auth files from the real HOME directory, bypassing AgentIM's
credential management entirely. This meant:
- No credential isolation between multiple subscription accounts
- Agents could use CLI credentials not managed by AgentIM
- Old credentials (created before oauthData support) appeared to work
  but without proper isolation

Now agentConfigToEnv() throws an explicit error when a subscription
credential lacks oauthData, directing the user to re-add the credential
via subscription login.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(m3): SVOD-specific metrics — QoE, content consumption, daily treatment effects, lifecycle segmentation (milestone 2.11) (#26)

Add four new SVOD-specific computation pipelines:

1. QoE Metric Computation: Aggregates playback telemetry from delta.qoe_events
   into per-user metric summaries (TTFF, rebuffer ratio). Uses separate SQL
   template reading from qoe_events instead of metric_events.

2. Content Consumption Distribution: Per-variant, per-content-item aggregation
   (watch_time, view_count, unique_viewers) written to delta.content_consumption.
   New ContentConsumptionJob orchestrator with its own job type.

3. Daily Treatment Effect Time Series: Post-processing step that reads from
   delta.metric_summaries to produce daily treatment-vs-control means for
   novelty/primacy detection. Writes to delta.daily_treatment_effects.

4. Lifecycle Segmentation: Computes metrics per lifecycle segment (TRIAL, NEW,
   ESTABLISHED, MATURE, AT_RISK, WINBACK) when experiment has
   lifecycle_stratification_enabled.

All SQL queries logged to query_log for transparency. Golden file tests verify
all 4 new templates. This unblocks Agent-4 (interference analysis needs
content_consumption, novelty analysis needs daily_treatment_effects).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Arbitrum Stylus GKR DAG verifier (Rust/WASM port)

Port the on-chain GKR DAG verifier from Solidity to Rust for Arbitrum
Stylus deployment, targeting ~25M gas (vs ~252M in EVM). Includes:
- BN254 field arithmetic (Fq/Fr) with schoolbook multiplication
- Poseidon sponge hash (width=3, rate=2, PSE/Scroll constants)
- Fiat-Shamir transcript setup matching ECTranscript protocol
- Committed sumcheck verification with PODP
- DAG compute layer verification (oracle expressions, point templates)
- Hyrax input layer verification (batch eval, MLE, claim grouping)
- Proof decoding from ABI-encoded bytes
- EC operations via EVM precompiles (ecadd/ecmul/sha256)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Harden against poisoned QR code XSS, add SRI hashes and audit logging

- Sanitize all innerHTML with escapeHtml() to prevent XSS from malicious
  SHL labels, FHIR resource types, PDF filenames, and app names
- Replace inline onclick download handlers with data-attribute delegation
- Add SRI (sha384) hashes to all CDN script tags (html5-qrcode, jose,
  pako, qrcode) for supply-chain protection
- Add cdn.jsdelivr.net to CSP script-src allowlist
- Create audit_log table and logAuditEvent() for non-PHI scan metadata
- Log every scan/route operation (org, storage type, counts, success,
  IP, user agent) — no patient data stored
- Add admin and super-admin audit log API endpoints
- Update architecture review response: XSS, SRI, audit trail all fixed
- Update security spec to v1.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement DAG Hybrid Groth16 verification pipeline and multi-tx batch verifier

- Add GKRDAGHybridVerifier.sol: Poseidon transcript replay + Groth16 EC checks for DAG circuits
- Add DAGBatchVerifier.sol: Multi-tx verification splitting 88-layer XGBoost across 15 txs (<30M gas each)
- Add DAGRemainderGroth16Verifier.sol: gnark-generated Groth16 verifier (3416 public inputs)
- Add gen_dag_groth16_witness.rs: Rust witness extractor for DAG topology with 34-group input strategy
- Add gnark dag_circuit.go + dag_main.go: DAG Groth16 wrapper circuit and prover
- Update RemainderVerifier.sol: verifyDAGWithGroth16() entry point, batch session management
- Fix input claim grouping to match Remainder_CE prover (numGroups == numClaims always)
- Add lTensor folding for multi-claim groups in EC verification
- 166 Solidity tests, 18 Go tests, 34 Rust tests all passing
